content.content:
1ŌåÆ# Architecture: Changelogue
     2ŌåÆ
     3ŌåÆ## Overview
     4ŌåÆ
     5ŌåÆChangelogue is an event-driven, hybrid-architecture system designed to centralize release discovery, automate validation, manage configurations, and distribute targeted notifications. It combines the high-concurrency performance of a Go-based polling engine with the reasoning capabilities of LLM-based SRE agents. By leveraging PostgreSQL for both persistent storage and message brokering, the system maintains high reliability and transactional consistency within a streamlined, single-binary deployment.
     6ŌåÆ
     7ŌåÆ## 1. Tech Stack & Infrastructure
     8ŌåÆ
     9ŌåÆ* **Backend / Engine:** Go (Golang). Chosen for its lightweight concurrency model (Goroutines) to handle simultaneous polling across multiple registries.
    10ŌåÆ* **Frontend / Dashboard:** Next.js (React) with Tailwind CSS. Provides a fast, modern control center for viewing release streams and managing configurations.
    11ŌåÆ* **Database, Queue, & Event Bus:** PostgreSQL.
    12ŌåÆ* *Persistent Storage:* Standard relational tables for release metadata and system configurations.
    13ŌåÆ* *Task Queue:* Utilizes `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` (via the **River** Go library) for robust, concurrent background job processing without race conditions.
    14ŌåÆ* *Pub/Sub:* Utilizes native `LISTEN` and `NOTIFY` for real-time broadcasting to connected clients.
    15ŌåÆ
    16ŌåÆ
    17ŌåÆ* **Intelligence Layer:** LLMs (Gemini / OpenAI-compatible) orchestrated via [ADK-Go](https://google.github.io/adk-go/) v0.5.0 for semantic changelog analysis and autonomous release evaluation.
    18ŌåÆ* **Packaging:** Single binary deployment utilizing Go's `//go:embed` to serve the Next.js static export directly from the Go server.
    19ŌåÆ
    20ŌåÆ## 2. System Design & Abstractions
    21ŌåÆ
    22ŌåÆThe system is decoupled into four primary layers communicating entirely through PostgreSQL using the Transactional Outbox pattern:
    23ŌåÆ
    24ŌåÆ1. **Ingestion Layer** ŌĆö Polling workers and webhook handlers discover new releases from upstream registries.
    25ŌåÆ2. **Notification Routing** ŌĆö River workers send immediate notifications to source-level subscribers on new releases.
    26ŌåÆ3. **Agent Layer** ŌĆö ADK-Go agents research releases, consult context sources, and produce semantic release reports.
    27ŌåÆ4. **Routing & Output** ŌĆö Notification channels (Slack, Discord, email, webhooks) deliver alerts via `Sender`.
    28ŌåÆ
    29ŌåÆA **Project** is the central domain entity ŌĆö it groups multiple ingestion sources, context sources, and notification subscriptions under a single tracked piece of software.
    30ŌåÆ
    31ŌåÆ```mermaid
    32ŌåÆgraph LR
    33ŌåÆ    %% Data Flow
    34ŌåÆ    subgraph Dashboard [API & Dashboard]
    35ŌåÆ        API[REST API] --> |CRUD| PG
    36ŌåÆ        API --> |SSE| Browser[Next.js Dashboard]
    37ŌåÆ    end
    38ŌåÆ
    39ŌåÆ    subgraph Ingestion [Ingestion Layer]
    40ŌåÆ        I_Hub(Docker Hub Poller) --> |IIngestionSource| Engine
    41ŌåÆ        I_Git(GitHub Atom Feed) --> |IIngestionSource| Engine
    42ŌåÆ    end
    43ŌåÆ
    44ŌåÆ    subgraph Core [Event Bus & Queue - PostgreSQL]
    45ŌåÆ        Engine((Go Engine)) --> |INSERT release + jobs\nTransactional Outbox| PG[(PostgreSQL)]
    46ŌåÆ    end
    47ŌåÆ
    48ŌåÆ    subgraph Notification [Notification Routing]
    49ŌåÆ        PG --> |SKIP LOCKED\nRiver notify_release| NotifyWorker[Notify Worker]
    50ŌåÆ        NotifyWorker --> |Sender| O_Slack(Slack)
    51ŌåÆ        NotifyWorker --> |Sender| O_Discord(Discord)
    52ŌåÆ        NotifyWorker --> |Sender| O_Email(Email)
    53ŌåÆ        NotifyWorker --> |Sender| O_Hook(Webhooks)
    54ŌåÆ    end
    55ŌåÆ
    56ŌåÆ    subgraph AgentLayer [Agent Layer]
    57ŌåÆ        PG --> |SKIP LOCKED\nRiver agent_run| AgentWorker[Agent Worker]
    58ŌåÆ        AgentWorker --> |ADK-Go| LLM[LLM Provider]
    59ŌåÆ        AgentWorker --> |Research| ContextSources[Context Sources]
    60ŌåÆ        AgentWorker --> |Produce| SemanticRelease[Semantic Release + Report]
    61ŌåÆ    end
    62ŌåÆ
    63ŌåÆ```
    64ŌåÆ
    65ŌåÆ### Entity Relationship Model
    66ŌåÆ
    67ŌåÆ```mermaid
    68ŌåÆerDiagram
    69ŌåÆ    projects ||--o{ sources : "has many"
    70ŌåÆ    projects ||--o{ context_sources : "has many"
    71ŌåÆ    projects ||--o{ semantic_releases : "has many"
    72ŌåÆ    projects ||--o{ agent_runs : "has many"
    73ŌåÆ    sources ||--o{ releases : "produces"
    74ŌåÆ    subscriptions }o--|| notification_channels : "routes to"
    75ŌåÆ    subscriptions }o--o| sources : "source-level"
    76ŌåÆ    subscriptions }o--o| projects : "project-level"
    77ŌåÆ    semantic_releases ||--o{ semantic_release_sources : "composed of"
    78ŌåÆ    releases ||--o{ semantic_release_sources : "contributes to"
    79ŌåÆ
    80ŌåÆ    projects {
    81ŌåÆ        uuid id PK
    82ŌåÆ        string name UK
    83ŌåÆ        text agent_prompt
    84ŌåÆ        jsonb agent_rules
    85ŌåÆ    }
    86ŌåÆ    sources {
    87ŌåÆ        uuid id PK
    88ŌåÆ        uuid project_id FK
    89ŌåÆ        string provider
    90ŌåÆ        string repository UK
    91ŌåÆ        boolean enabled
    92ŌåÆ    }
    93ŌåÆ    releases {
    94ŌåÆ        uuid id PK
    95ŌåÆ        uuid source_id FK
    96ŌåÆ        string version
    97ŌåÆ        jsonb raw_data
    98ŌåÆ    }
    99ŌåÆ    semantic_releases {
   100ŌåÆ        uuid id PK
   101ŌåÆ        uuid project_id FK
   102ŌåÆ        string version
   103ŌåÆ        jsonb report
   104ŌåÆ        string status
   105ŌåÆ    }
   106ŌåÆ    agent_runs {
   107ŌåÆ        uuid id PK
   108ŌåÆ        uuid project_id FK
   109ŌåÆ        uuid semantic_release_id FK
   110ŌåÆ        string trigger
   111ŌåÆ        string status
   112ŌåÆ    }
   113ŌåÆ    subscriptions {
   114ŌåÆ        uuid id PK
   115ŌåÆ        uuid channel_id FK
   116ŌåÆ        string type
   117ŌåÆ        uuid source_id FK
   118ŌåÆ        uuid project_id FK
   119ŌåÆ    }
   120ŌåÆ    notification_channels {
   121ŌåÆ        uuid id PK
   122ŌåÆ        string type
   123ŌåÆ        string name
   124ŌåÆ        jsonb config
   125ŌåÆ    }
   126ŌåÆ```
   127ŌåÆ
   128ŌåÆ### 2.1 The Event-Driven Backbone (PostgreSQL Only)
   129ŌåÆ
   130ŌåÆComponents do not call each other synchronously. Instead, they rely on PostgreSQL to guarantee delivery:
   131ŌåÆ
   132ŌåÆ* **The Transactional Outbox:** When a new release is detected, the ingestion worker writes the metadata to the `releases` table (linked to its `source_id`) and simultaneously enqueues River jobs (notification and/or agent) *within the exact same SQL transaction*. This guarantees no events are ever lost.
   133ŌåÆ* **Real-time Pub/Sub:** Database triggers use `pg_notify` to broadcast lightweight events (e.g., telling the Next.js UI via SSE to refresh) over standard Postgres connections using `LISTEN`.
   134ŌåÆ* **Reliable Queues:** River workers process jobs using `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`, ensuring exactly-once processing for both notification delivery and agent runs.
   135ŌåÆ* **Project-Centric Model:** All data flows through the `projects` ŌåÆ `sources` ŌåÆ `releases` hierarchy. Subscriptions can attach at the source level (for raw release notifications) or the project level (for semantic release notifications).
   136ŌåÆ
   137ŌåÆ### 2.2 Provider Interfaces (I/O)
   138ŌåÆ
   139ŌåÆAll external integrations are abstracted behind strict Go interfaces.
   140ŌåÆ
   141ŌåÆ* `IIngestionSource`: Standardizes how polling workers fetch data. Adding a new registry (like npm or NuGet) only requires implementing this interface. Each implementation maps to a `source_type` in the database. Current implementations: Docker Hub, GitHub, ECR Public, GitLab, PyPI.
   142ŌåÆ* `Sender`: Standardizes output routing. Each implementation maps to a `type` in the `notification_channels` table (Slack, Discord, email, webhooks).
   143ŌåÆ
   144ŌåÆ### 2.3 REST API & Dashboard
   145ŌåÆ
   146ŌåÆThe Go server exposes a RESTful API (`/api/v1`) serving the Next.js dashboard and external consumers:
   147ŌåÆ
   148ŌåÆ* **Resource CRUD:** Projects, sources, subscriptions, notification channels, context sources ŌĆö all manageable through the API.
   149ŌåÆ* **Read-Only Releases:** Releases and semantic releases are queryable but not directly writable via the API ŌĆö they're created exclusively through the ingestion layer and agent runs.
   150ŌåÆ* **Source Operations:** Manual poll trigger (`POST /sources/{id}/poll`) and channel test notifications (`POST /channels/{id}/test`).
   151ŌåÆ* **SSE Real-Time Events:** `GET /api/v1/events` streams server-sent events backed by PostgreSQL `LISTEN/NOTIFY`, pushing release and semantic release updates to connected dashboard clients.
   152ŌåÆ* **API Key Auth:** Bearer token authentication with hashed key storage. `NO_AUTH=true` disables auth for development.
   153ŌåÆ* **Rate Limiting:** Per-key token bucket (10 rps, 20 burst) with standard `X-Ratelimit-*` response headers.
   154ŌåÆ* **Stats & Trends:** Dashboard stats and time-bucketed release count trends via `/stats` and `/stats/trend`.
   155ŌåÆ
   156ŌåÆSee [API.md](API.md) for the full endpoint reference.
   157ŌåÆ
   158ŌåÆ### 2.4 Notification Routing
   159ŌåÆ
   160ŌåÆWhen a new source release is detected, a `notify_release` River job is enqueued in the same transaction as the release insert. The notification worker:
   161ŌåÆ
   162ŌåÆ1. Resolves all source-level subscriptions for the release's source.
   163ŌåÆ2. For each subscription, sends a notification to the linked channel via `Sender`.
   164ŌåÆ3. Checks the project's agent rules ŌĆö if the release matches (major bump, security patch, version pattern), enqueues an `agent_run` job automatically.
   165ŌåÆ
   166ŌåÆ### 2.5 Agent Layer (Implemented)
   167ŌåÆ
   168ŌåÆFor project-level intelligence, an `agent_run` River job triggers an LLM agent (via ADK-Go v0.5.0) that:
   169ŌåÆ
   170ŌåÆ1. Gathers recent source releases for the project using the `get_releases` tool.
   171ŌåÆ2. Inspects individual release details (changelogs, raw data) using `get_release_detail`.
   172ŌåÆ3. Consults context sources (runbooks, docs, monitoring dashboards) using `list_context_sources`.
   173ŌåÆ4. Produces a `SemanticRelease` with a structured `SemanticReport` (subject, risk level, changelog summary, status checks, download commands, urgency, recommendation).
   174ŌåÆ
   175ŌåÆAgent behavior is configured per-project via `agent_prompt` (custom instructions) and `agent_rules` (structured triggers like `on_major_release`, `on_security_patch`, `version_pattern`, `wait_for_all_sources`). Agent runs are tracked in the `agent_runs` table for observability and auditability.
   176ŌåÆ
   177ŌåÆ**LLM Provider Support:**
   178ŌåÆ
   179ŌåÆThe agent supports multiple LLM backends via the `LLM_PROVIDER` environment variable:
   180ŌåÆ* **Gemini** (default) ŌĆö Uses `GOOGLE_API_KEY` with ADK-Go's native Gemini model integration.
   181ŌåÆ* **OpenAI** ŌĆö Uses `OPENAI_API_KEY` and `OPENAI_BASE_URL` via an OpenAI-compatible adapter (`internal/agent/openai/`).
   182ŌåÆ
   183ŌåÆ**Implementation details (`internal/agent/`):**
   184ŌåÆ
   185ŌåÆ* **`tools.go`** ŌĆö Three ADK function tools (`get_releases`, `get_release_detail`, `list_context_sources`) created via `functiontool.New()`. Tools are scoped to a project via `toolFactory` which holds the `AgentDataStore` and project ID.
   186ŌåÆ* **`orchestrator.go`** ŌĆö `Orchestrator` manages the full agent lifecycle: loading project config, creating an ADK `llmagent` with the configured LLM backend, running the agent via `runner.Runner`, parsing the JSON report, and persisting the `SemanticRelease` in a transaction.
   187ŌåÆ* **`worker.go`** ŌĆö `AgentWorker` implements `river.Worker[queue.AgentJobArgs]`, loading the agent run from the store and delegating to `Orchestrator.RunAgent()`.
   188ŌåÆ* **Graceful degradation** ŌĆö If no LLM API key is set, the orchestrator is not created, the worker is not registered, and a warning is logged. The rest of the system continues to function. Agent jobs remain in the River queue until the key is configured.
   189ŌåÆ
   190ŌåÆ### 2.6 Agentic Tooling (Planned: SRE Validation)
   191ŌåÆ
   192ŌåÆFor deep validation, Changelogue will utilize SRE agents with a suite of abstracted tools:
   193ŌåÆ
   194ŌåÆ* `UpgradeBaseABoxConfig(version)`
   195ŌåÆ* `CheckAgentStatus(environment)`
   196ŌåÆ* `SyncOpsOpsack(payload)`
   197ŌåÆThis will allow the agent to autonomously deploy a sandbox, verify that the deployment is healthy, and rollback or alert on failure.
   198ŌåÆ
   199ŌåÆ## 3. Data Flow: Lifecycle of a Release Event
   200ŌåÆ
   201ŌåÆ1. **Discovery:** A Go worker polling Docker Hub detects a new base image tag for a configured source (e.g., the "Docker Hub" source under the "Go Runtime" project). Source-level exclusion filters (`exclude_version_regexp`, `exclude_prereleases`) are applied ŌĆö filtered versions are discarded immediately.
   202ŌåÆ2. **Ingestion & Transaction:** The worker stores the raw upstream payload and executes a single database transaction to insert the record into `releases` (linked to its `source_id`) and enqueue a `notify_release` River job.
   203ŌåÆ3. **Notification (Source-Level):** A River worker picks up the `notify_release` job, resolves source-level subscriptions, and sends alerts to configured notification channels (Slack, Discord, email, webhooks).
   204ŌåÆ4. **Agent Rule Check:** The same notification worker evaluates the project's `agent_rules` against the new release version. If criteria match (major bump, security patch, version pattern), it enqueues an `agent_run` River job.
   205ŌåÆ5. **Agent Analysis (Project-Level):** A River worker picks up the `agent_run` job. The ADK-Go agent gathers recent releases, consults context sources, and produces a `SemanticRelease` with a structured report (subject, risk level, changelog summary, status checks, urgency, recommendation).
   206ŌåÆ6. **Broadcast:** PostgreSQL triggers fire `NOTIFY` payloads on release insert and semantic release completion, pushing SSE events to connected dashboard clients.
   207ŌåÆ7. **Project-Level Notification:** When a semantic release is completed, project-level subscribers are notified with the AI-generated report.
   208ŌåÆ
   209ŌåÆ## 4. Directory Structure
   210ŌåÆ
   211ŌåÆ```text
   212ŌåÆ/changelogue
   213ŌåÆŌö£ŌöĆŌöĆ cmd/
   214ŌåÆŌöé   Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ server/          # Main Go application entry point
   215ŌåÆŌöé   ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ agent/           # Agent CLI ŌĆö run agent analysis for a project
   216ŌåÆŌö£ŌöĆŌöĆ internal/
   217ŌåÆŌöé   Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ api/             # REST API handlers, middleware, SSE broadcaster
   218ŌåÆŌöé   Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ ingestion/       # Polling workers (IIngestionSource: Docker Hub, GitHub, ECR Public, GitLab, PyPI)
   219ŌåÆŌöé   Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ agent/           # ADK-Go agent for semantic release analysis
   220ŌåÆŌöé   Ōöé   ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ openai/      # OpenAI-compatible LLM provider adapter
   221ŌåÆŌöé   Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ routing/         # Notification routing worker and channel implementations (Sender)
   222ŌåÆŌöé   Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ queue/           # River queue job definitions (NotifyJobArgs, AgentJobArgs)
   223ŌåÆŌöé   Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ db/              # Connection pool and schema migrations
   224ŌåÆŌöé   ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ models/          # Shared domain structs (Release, Project, SemanticRelease, etc.)
   225ŌåÆŌö£ŌöĆŌöĆ web/                 # Next.js frontend application
   226ŌåÆŌöé   Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ app/             # App Router pages (dashboard, projects, releases, channels, etc.)
   227ŌåÆŌöé   Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ components/      # React components organized by domain
   228ŌåÆŌöé   ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ lib/             # Client utilities and API wrappers
   229ŌåÆŌö£ŌöĆŌöĆ scripts/             # Integration test harness
   230ŌåÆŌö£ŌöĆŌöĆ docs/                # Design documents and plans
   231ŌåÆŌööŌöĆŌöĆ go.mod
   232ŌåÆ```

Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

content.text:
I now have a thorough understanding of the codebase and the spec. Let me compile the review.

---

## **Issues Found**

### Critical Issues

**1. NO_AUTH / dev mode makes the suggestions endpoints non-functional.**

In `NO_AUTH` mode, the `chain` middleware is `publicChain` (no auth), meaning `auth.UserFromContext(r.Context())` returns `nil` -- there is no user in context. The spec says the new endpoints use "Auth required (session cookie)" and will extract `github_login` from the session user, but it does not address how this works in `NO_AUTH` mode. The existing `Auth` middleware is entirely skipped when `deps.NoAuth` is true (see `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/api/server.go` line 42-45). The dev user has `GitHubLogin: "dev"`, and calling `https://api.github.com/users/dev/starred` would return some random GitHub user's stars, not the developer's.

**Recommendation**: The spec must explicitly state how the suggestions routes behave in `NO_AUTH` mode. Options: (a) register them on `chain` (auth-required) even in `NO_AUTH` mode -- but then they fail since `chain == publicChain`; (b) add special-case handling in the handler to check if user is nil or login is "dev" and return an empty result or an error; (c) use the `DevSession` middleware wrapper for these specific routes in `NO_AUTH` mode. This needs to be specified.

**2. The `/api/v1/suggestions/stars` endpoint relies on starred repos being public, but the GitHub API endpoint `GET /users/{username}/starred` only returns *public* stars -- this is correct for a server-side token, but this limitation is not documented.**

The spec says "Public GitHub API" and "no per-user token storage", which means the server-side `GITHUB_TOKEN` authenticates the request for rate-limit purposes, but since it is not the user's own token, only the user's publicly-visible stars are returned. If a user has starred private repos, those will not appear. This is probably fine but should be explicitly called out as a known limitation.

**3. The `/api/v1/suggestions/repos` endpoint uses `GET /users/{github_login}/repos` which only returns the user's *public* repositories.**

Same issue as above. The `GET /users/{username}/repos` endpoint only lists public repos when authenticated with a different user's token. The spec should document this explicitly. More importantly: for the dependency scanning use case, users likely want to scan their *private* repos too (that is where most real dependencies live). The spec should acknowledge this limitation clearly in the "Non-goals" section or "Edge Cases."

**4. The spec says "Filters out repos already tracked as sources" for the stars endpoint but does not specify the mechanism.**

The handler needs access to the `SourcesStore` to query which repositories are already tracked. The spec lists `internal/api/suggestions.go` as the new file with "two handlers + GitHub API client functions + caching" but does not mention that the handler needs a `SourcesStore` dependency. Looking at how the `DiscoverHandler` is instantiated in `server.go` (line 134: `discover := NewDiscoverHandler(deps.HTTPClient, "", "")`), the suggestions handler will need more dependencies.

**Recommendation**: The spec should state that `NewSuggestionsHandler` takes `deps.HTTPClient`, `deps.SourcesStore`, and optionally a `GITHUB_TOKEN` string.

### Moderate Issues

**5. Missing specification of how to extract `github_login` from the request context in the handler.**

The spec says "Auth required (session cookie)" but does not specify that the handler calls `auth.UserFromContext(r.Context())` to get the `*auth.User` and reads `user.GitHubLogin`. This is an implementation detail, but since the spec enumerates the exact GitHub API URLs with `{github_login}`, it should state where that value comes from in the handler. More critically, when the request comes via Bearer token (API key auth), there is **no user in context** at all -- the `Auth` middleware (line 36 in `auth.go`) calls `next.ServeHTTP(w, r)` without injecting a user. API key users cannot use these endpoints. The spec should note this.

**6. The response format for `/api/v1/suggestions/stars` does not wrap in the standard `ApiResponse` envelope.**

Looking at the existing API client (`/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/lib/api/client.ts`), all responses use `ApiResponse<T>` which wraps data in `{ data: T }`. The spec's response description says "array of `{ name, full_name, ... }`" but doesn't clarify whether it follows the standard envelope pattern. The frontend client modifications listed (`suggestions.stars()` and `suggestions.repos()`) need to know the response shape.

**Recommendation**: Explicitly state the response uses `ApiResponse<[]DiscoverItem>` format consistent with other endpoints, or define a new response type.

**7. The spec says "Already-tracked repos dimmed with checkmark" in StarsTab but the filtering happens both server-side ("Filters out repos already tracked as sources") and client-side ("Already-tracked repos dimmed").**

These are contradictory. If the server filters them out, the client will never see them to dim. If the intent is to dim rather than hide, the server should return them with a `tracked: true` flag instead of filtering. The spec needs to pick one approach.

**8. Missing pagination details for the `/api/v1/suggestions/repos` endpoint.**

The stars endpoint says "Paginates up to ~100 results (GitHub returns 30/page)" which implies the server fetches multiple pages from GitHub and aggregates. The repos endpoint just says `per_page=100` in the URL. The spec should clarify: does the server do multi-page fetching for repos too? GitHub caps `per_page` at 100, so users with more than 100 repos will see a truncated list. Is that acceptable? Should there be cursor-based pagination on the Changelogue API side?

**9. Cache invalidation not addressed.**

The spec says "Caches per-user for 1 hour" for both endpoints. But if a user tracks a repo from the stars list, the cached results will still show that repo for up to an hour (even if server-side filtering is applied, the cache would need to be invalidated or the filtering must happen post-cache). The spec should clarify whether filtering happens before or after the cache, or whether tracking a repo invalidates the cache.

### Minor Issues

**10. The spec says the frontend falls back to `DiscoverySection` for "unauthenticated users" but the auth context in `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/lib/auth/context.tsx` redirects unauthenticated users to `/login` (line 65).** 

If unauthenticated users are always redirected to `/login`, they will never see the dashboard page at all. The "unauthenticated fallback" scenario only applies in `NO_AUTH` dev mode (where `user` is always set). The spec's framing about "unauthenticated users see existing trending carousel" is misleading given the actual auth flow.

**Recommendation**: Reframe as "Users without a GitHub login (e.g., API key auth or dev mode) see the existing trending carousel."

**11. The "Files to Create/Modify" section does not mention adding `GITHUB_TOKEN` to the `SuggestionsHandler` initialization.**

The discover handler currently does not use `GITHUB_TOKEN`. The ingestion layer and onboard scanner read it from `os.Getenv` directly. The spec should clarify whether the suggestions handler also reads from env directly (like the onboard scanner) or receives it as a constructor parameter (more testable).

**12. No error response format specified.**

The spec says "Show friendly error if rate limited" for GitHub rate limiting but does not specify the HTTP status code or error response body the Changelogue API should return. Looking at existing patterns in `discover.go`, the convention is `RespondError(w, r, http.StatusBadGateway, "upstream_error", ...)`. The spec should match this.

**13. The spec does not mention tests.**

No test file is listed in the "Files to Create/Modify" section. Given the existing `discover_test.go` likely exists and the pattern of thorough testing in the codebase, the spec should call out `internal/api/suggestions_test.go` as a new file.

---

**Verdict: Issues Found** -- 13 issues identified. Issues 1, 4, 5, and 7 are the most important to resolve before implementation, as they would cause confusion or bugs during development. The rest are specification gaps that a senior implementer could figure out from context but would benefit from explicit documentation.

content.text:
I now have a thorough understanding of the codebase and the spec. Let me compile the review.

---

## **Issues Found**

### Critical Issues

**1. NO_AUTH / dev mode makes the suggestions endpoints non-functional.**

In `NO_AUTH` mode, the `chain` middleware is `publicChain` (no auth), meaning `auth.UserFromContext(r.Context())` returns `nil` -- there is no user in context. The spec says the new endpoints use "Auth required (session cookie)" and will extract `github_login` from the session user, but it does not address how this works in `NO_AUTH` mode. The existing `Auth` middleware is entirely skipped when `deps.NoAuth` is true (see `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/api/server.go` line 42-45). The dev user has `GitHubLogin: "dev"`, and calling `https://api.github.com/users/dev/starred` would return some random GitHub user's stars, not the developer's.

**Recommendation**: The spec must explicitly state how the suggestions routes behave in `NO_AUTH` mode. Options: (a) register them on `chain` (auth-required) even in `NO_AUTH` mode -- but then they fail since `chain == publicChain`; (b) add special-case handling in the handler to check if user is nil or login is "dev" and return an empty result or an error; (c) use the `DevSession` middleware wrapper for these specific routes in `NO_AUTH` mode. This needs to be specified.

**2. The `/api/v1/suggestions/stars` endpoint relies on starred repos being public, but the GitHub API endpoint `GET /users/{username}/starred` only returns *public* stars -- this is correct for a server-side token, but this limitation is not documented.**

The spec says "Public GitHub API" and "no per-user token storage", which means the server-side `GITHUB_TOKEN` authenticates the request for rate-limit purposes, but since it is not the user's own token, only the user's publicly-visible stars are returned. If a user has starred private repos, those will not appear. This is probably fine but should be explicitly called out as a known limitation.

**3. The `/api/v1/suggestions/repos` endpoint uses `GET /users/{github_login}/repos` which only returns the user's *public* repositories.**

Same issue as above. The `GET /users/{username}/repos` endpoint only lists public repos when authenticated with a different user's token. The spec should document this explicitly. More importantly: for the dependency scanning use case, users likely want to scan their *private* repos too (that is where most real dependencies live). The spec should acknowledge this limitation clearly in the "Non-goals" section or "Edge Cases."

**4. The spec says "Filters out repos already tracked as sources" for the stars endpoint but does not specify the mechanism.**

The handler needs access to the `SourcesStore` to query which repositories are already tracked. The spec lists `internal/api/suggestions.go` as the new file with "two handlers + GitHub API client functions + caching" but does not mention that the handler needs a `SourcesStore` dependency. Looking at how the `DiscoverHandler` is instantiated in `server.go` (line 134: `discover := NewDiscoverHandler(deps.HTTPClient, "", "")`), the suggestions handler will need more dependencies.

**Recommendation**: The spec should state that `NewSuggestionsHandler` takes `deps.HTTPClient`, `deps.SourcesStore`, and optionally a `GITHUB_TOKEN` string.

### Moderate Issues

**5. Missing specification of how to extract `github_login` from the request context in the handler.**

The spec says "Auth required (session cookie)" but does not specify that the handler calls `auth.UserFromContext(r.Context())` to get the `*auth.User` and reads `user.GitHubLogin`. This is an implementation detail, but since the spec enumerates the exact GitHub API URLs with `{github_login}`, it should state where that value comes from in the handler. More critically, when the request comes via Bearer token (API key auth), there is **no user in context** at all -- the `Auth` middleware (line 36 in `auth.go`) calls `next.ServeHTTP(w, r)` without injecting a user. API key users cannot use these endpoints. The spec should note this.

**6. The response format for `/api/v1/suggestions/stars` does not wrap in the standard `ApiResponse` envelope.**

Looking at the existing API client (`/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/lib/api/client.ts`), all responses use `ApiResponse<T>` which wraps data in `{ data: T }`. The spec's response description says "array of `{ name, full_name, ... }`" but doesn't clarify whether it follows the standard envelope pattern. The frontend client modifications listed (`suggestions.stars()` and `suggestions.repos()`) need to know the response shape.

**Recommendation**: Explicitly state the response uses `ApiResponse<[]DiscoverItem>` format consistent with other endpoints, or define a new response type.

**7. The spec says "Already-tracked repos dimmed with checkmark" in StarsTab but the filtering happens both server-side ("Filters out repos already tracked as sources") and client-side ("Already-tracked repos dimmed").**

These are contradictory. If the server filters them out, the client will never see them to dim. If the intent is to dim rather than hide, the server should return them with a `tracked: true` flag instead of filtering. The spec needs to pick one approach.

**8. Missing pagination details for the `/api/v1/suggestions/repos` endpoint.**

The stars endpoint says "Paginates up to ~100 results (GitHub returns 30/page)" which implies the server fetches multiple pages from GitHub and aggregates. The repos endpoint just says `per_page=100` in the URL. The spec should clarify: does the server do multi-page fetching for repos too? GitHub caps `per_page` at 100, so users with more than 100 repos will see a truncated list. Is that acceptable? Should there be cursor-based pagination on the Changelogue API side?

**9. Cache invalidation not addressed.**

The spec says "Caches per-user for 1 hour" for both endpoints. But if a user tracks a repo from the stars list, the cached results will still show that repo for up to an hour (even if server-side filtering is applied, the cache would need to be invalidated or the filtering must happen post-cache). The spec should clarify whether filtering happens before or after the cache, or whether tracking a repo invalidates the cache.

### Minor Issues

**10. The spec says the frontend falls back to `DiscoverySection` for "unauthenticated users" but the auth context in `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/lib/auth/context.tsx` redirects unauthenticated users to `/login` (line 65).** 

If unauthenticated users are always redirected to `/login`, they will never see the dashboard page at all. The "unauthenticated fallback" scenario only applies in `NO_AUTH` dev mode (where `user` is always set). The spec's framing about "unauthenticated users see existing trending carousel" is misleading given the actual auth flow.

**Recommendation**: Reframe as "Users without a GitHub login (e.g., API key auth or dev mode) see the existing trending carousel."

**11. The "Files to Create/Modify" section does not mention adding `GITHUB_TOKEN` to the `SuggestionsHandler` initialization.**

The discover handler currently does not use `GITHUB_TOKEN`. The ingestion layer and onboard scanner read it from `os.Getenv` directly. The spec should clarify whether the suggestions handler also reads from env directly (like the onboard scanner) or receives it as a constructor parameter (more testable).

**12. No error response format specified.**

The spec says "Show friendly error if rate limited" for GitHub rate limiting but does not specify the HTTP status code or error response body the Changelogue API should return. Looking at existing patterns in `discover.go`, the convention is `RespondError(w, r, http.StatusBadGateway, "upstream_error", ...)`. The spec should match this.

**13. The spec does not mention tests.**

No test file is listed in the "Files to Create/Modify" section. Given the existing `discover_test.go` likely exists and the pattern of thorough testing in the codebase, the spec should call out `internal/api/suggestions_test.go` as a new file.

---

**Verdict: Issues Found** -- 13 issues identified. Issues 1, 4, 5, and 7 are the most important to resolve before implementation, as they would cause confusion or bugs during development. The rest are specification gaps that a senior implementer could figure out from context but would benefit from explicit documentation.
Good review feedback. Let me fix the key issues in the spec.

# Personalized GitHub Suggestions ŌĆö Design Spec

## Overview

Add personalized repo/dependency suggestions to the dashboard for GitHub-authenticated users. Replaces the generic trending discovery carousel with two tabs: "Your Stars" (user's starred repos) and "Your Dependencies" (scan user-selected repos for dependencies). Uses public GitHub API only ŌĆö no OAuth changes needed.

## Decisions

- **Data access**: Public GitHub API via server-side `GITHUB_TOKEN` (no per-user token storage, no OAuth scope changes)
- **Placement**: Replaces trending carousel for authenticated users; unauthenticated users see existing trending carousel
- **Trigger**: On demand ŌĆö user clicks to fetch each tab's data
- **Layout**: Two tabs ŌĆö "Your Stars" | "Your Dependencies"
- **Dependencies flow**: User picks repos from checklist ŌåÆ scan selected ŌåÆ review & track results
- **Dependency scanning**: Reuses existing onboard scan pipeline (LLM extraction via `POST /api/v1/onboard/scan`)

## Backend API

### `GET /api/v1/suggestions/stars`

- Auth required (session cookie)
- Fetches `GET https://api.github.com/users/{github_login}/starred` using server-side `GITHUB_TOKEN`
- Paginates up to ~100 results (GitHub returns 30/page)
- Caches per-user for 1 hour (keyed by `github_login`)
- Filters out repos already tracked as sources
- Response: array of `{ name, full_name, description, stars, language, url, avatar_url, provider: "github" }`

### `GET /api/v1/suggestions/repos`

- Auth required (session cookie)
- Fetches `GET https://api.github.com/users/{github_login}/repos?sort=pushed&per_page=100`
- Caches per-user for 1 hour
- Response: array of `{ name, full_name, description, language, url, pushed_at }`

### Dependency scanning

Reuses existing endpoints ŌĆö no new endpoints needed:
- `POST /api/v1/onboard/scan` ŌĆö start scan for a repo
- `GET /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}` ŌĆö poll scan status
- `POST /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}/apply` ŌĆö apply selections (create projects + sources)

## Frontend Components

### SuggestionsSection

Top-level component that replaces `DiscoverySection` for authenticated users. Manages tab state ("stars" | "deps"). Falls back to `DiscoverySection` for unauthenticated users.

### StarsTab

- Initial state: "Load your starred repos" button
- After fetch: grid of repo cards with name, description, star count, language badge
- Already-tracked repos dimmed with checkmark
- One-click "Track" button per repo (creates project + source + auto-polls, same logic as discovery carousel)
- "Show more" pagination

### DepsTab

Multi-step flow:

1. **Repo picker**: "Load your repos" button ŌåÆ fetches repo list ŌåÆ checkboxes sorted by `pushed_at` desc ŌåÆ "Scan Selected (N)" button
2. **Scanning**: Progress indicators per repo (reuses onboard progress UI patterns). Cap at 5 concurrent scans.
3. **Results**: Dependencies grouped by source repo (e.g., "From myapp ŌåÆ express, lodash, pg"). Checkboxes to select which to track. Dropdown to assign to existing project or create new one.
4. **Applied**: Success confirmation with counts (reuses onboard applied state)

## Edge Cases

- **GitHub rate limiting**: 5000 req/hr with server-side token. Cache 1hr per user. Show friendly error if rate limited.
- **No stars / no repos**: Empty state messages with suggestion to try trending discovery or quick onboard instead.
- **Scan failures**: Per-repo status (success/failed/no deps). Failed scans don't block others.
- **Already tracked**: Both tabs filter/dim repos and dependencies already tracked as sources.
- **Concurrent scans**: Cap at 5 concurrent per user. Existing 409 conflict handling for duplicate repo scans.

## Files to Create/Modify

### New files
- `internal/api/suggestions.go` ŌĆö two handlers + GitHub API client functions + caching
- `web/components/dashboard/suggestions-section.tsx` ŌĆö top-level tabbed component
- `web/components/dashboard/stars-tab.tsx` ŌĆö stars tab with repo cards
- `web/components/dashboard/deps-tab.tsx` ŌĆö dependencies tab with multi-step flow

### Modified files
- `internal/api/server.go` ŌĆö register new `/api/v1/suggestions/` routes
- `web/lib/api/client.ts` ŌĆö add `suggestions.stars()` and `suggestions.repos()` client methods
- `web/app/page.tsx` ŌĆö conditionally render `SuggestionsSection` vs `DiscoverySection` based on auth state

## Non-goals (future work)

- Private repo access (requires storing per-user OAuth tokens, expanded scopes)
- Auto-fetch on first login
- Recommendation ranking / ML-based suggestions
- Non-GitHub providers (GitLab, Bitbucket stars/repos)

# Personalized GitHub Suggestions ŌĆö Design Spec

## Overview

Add personalized repo/dependency suggestions to the dashboard for GitHub-authenticated users. Replaces the generic trending discovery carousel with two tabs: "Your Stars" (user's starred repos) and "Your Dependencies" (scan user-selected repos for dependencies). Uses public GitHub API only ŌĆö no OAuth changes needed.

## Decisions

- **Data access**: Public GitHub API via server-side `GITHUB_TOKEN` (no per-user token storage, no OAuth scope changes)
- **Placement**: Replaces trending carousel for authenticated users; unauthenticated users see existing trending carousel
- **Trigger**: On demand ŌĆö user clicks to fetch each tab's data
- **Layout**: Two tabs ŌĆö "Your Stars" | "Your Dependencies"
- **Dependencies flow**: User picks repos from checklist ŌåÆ scan selected ŌåÆ review & track results
- **Dependency scanning**: Reuses existing onboard scan pipeline (LLM extraction via `POST /api/v1/onboard/scan`)

## Known Limitations

- **Public data only**: `GET /users/{username}/starred` and `GET /users/{username}/repos` with a server-side token only return publicly visible stars and public repos. Private stars and private repos are not accessible. This is by design for the current scope ŌĆö private repo support is a future goal requiring per-user token storage.
- **Repo cap**: GitHub API caps `per_page` at 100. Users with 100+ repos see only the most recently pushed 100. Acceptable for now.

## Backend API

### Handler Construction

`NewSuggestionsHandler(httpClient *http.Client, sourcesStore db.SourcesStore, githubToken string)` ŌĆö reads `GITHUB_TOKEN` from env at initialization (same pattern as onboard scanner). Needs `SourcesStore` dependency to check which repos are already tracked.

### `GET /api/v1/suggestions/stars`

- Auth required: extracts user via `auth.UserFromContext(r.Context())`, reads `user.GitHubLogin`
- In `NO_AUTH` mode or when `github_login` is empty/`"dev"`: returns empty array (not an error)
- API key auth (Bearer token): returns 403 ŌĆö these endpoints require a session with a GitHub identity
- Fetches `GET https://api.github.com/users/{github_login}/starred` using server-side `GITHUB_TOKEN`
- Paginates up to ~100 results (GitHub returns 30/page, fetch up to 4 pages)
- Caches raw GitHub response per-user for 1 hour (keyed by `github_login`). Tracked-repo filtering applied post-cache so tracking a repo is reflected immediately without cache invalidation.
- Returns already-tracked repos with a `tracked: true` flag (not filtered out) so the frontend can dim them with a checkmark
- Response: `ApiResponse<[]SuggestionItem>` where `SuggestionItem` is `{ name, full_name, description, stars, language, url, avatar_url, provider: "github", tracked: bool }`
- Rate limit error: returns `502 Bad Gateway` with `{ error: "upstream_error", message: "GitHub API rate limit exceeded, try again later" }`

### `GET /api/v1/suggestions/repos`

- Same auth requirements as `/stars`
- Fetches `GET https://api.github.com/users/{github_login}/repos?sort=pushed&per_page=100`
- Caches per-user for 1 hour
- Response: `ApiResponse<[]RepoItem>` where `RepoItem` is `{ name, full_name, description, language, url, pushed_at }`

### Dependency scanning

Reuses existing endpoints ŌĆö no new endpoints needed:
- `POST /api/v1/onboard/scan` ŌĆö start scan for a repo
- `GET /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}` ŌĆö poll scan status
- `POST /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}/apply` ŌĆö apply selections (create projects + sources)

## Frontend Components

### SuggestionsSection

Top-level component that replaces `DiscoverySection` for users with a `github_login`. Manages tab state ("stars" | "deps"). Falls back to `DiscoverySection` when `github_login` is absent (dev mode, API key auth).

### StarsTab

- Initial state: "Load your starred repos" button
- After fetch: grid of repo cards with name, description, star count, language badge
- Already-tracked repos dimmed with checkmark (using `tracked` flag from API)
- One-click "Track" button per repo (creates project + source + auto-polls, same logic as discovery carousel)
- "Show more" pagination (client-side, data already fetched)

### DepsTab

Multi-step flow:

1. **Repo picker**: "Load your repos" button ŌåÆ fetches repo list ŌåÆ checkboxes sorted by `pushed_at` desc ŌåÆ "Scan Selected (N)" button
2. **Scanning**: Progress indicators per repo (reuses onboard progress UI patterns). Cap at 5 concurrent scans.
3. **Results**: Dependencies grouped by source repo (e.g., "From myapp ŌåÆ express, lodash, pg"). Checkboxes to select which to track. Dropdown to assign to existing project or create new one.
4. **Applied**: Success confirmation with counts (reuses onboard applied state)

## Edge Cases

- **GitHub rate limiting**: 5000 req/hr with server-side token. Cache 1hr per user. Return 502 with descriptive error if rate limited.
- **No stars / no repos**: Empty state messages with suggestion to try trending discovery or quick onboard instead.
- **Scan failures**: Per-repo status (success/failed/no deps). Failed scans don't block others.
- **Already tracked**: Stars tab returns `tracked: true` flag; frontend dims with checkmark. Deps tab results similarly indicate already-tracked dependencies.
- **Concurrent scans**: Cap at 5 concurrent per user. Existing 409 conflict handling for duplicate repo scans.
- **NO_AUTH / dev mode**: Suggestions endpoints return empty arrays when `github_login` is empty or `"dev"`. Frontend shows trending carousel fallback.
- **Cache vs. tracking**: GitHub API response is cached; tracked-repo filtering is applied post-cache so it reflects immediately.

## Files to Create/Modify

### New files
- `internal/api/suggestions.go` ŌĆö `SuggestionsHandler` with `handleStars`, `handleRepos` + GitHub API fetching + per-user caching
- `internal/api/suggestions_test.go` ŌĆö unit tests for handlers (mock GitHub API, mock SourcesStore)
- `web/components/dashboard/suggestions-section.tsx` ŌĆö top-level tabbed component
- `web/components/dashboard/stars-tab.tsx` ŌĆö stars tab with repo cards
- `web/components/dashboard/deps-tab.tsx` ŌĆö dependencies tab with multi-step flow

### Modified files
- `internal/api/server.go` ŌĆö register new `/api/v1/suggestions/` routes on auth-required chain, construct `SuggestionsHandler` with `deps.SourcesStore` and `GITHUB_TOKEN`
- `web/lib/api/client.ts` ŌĆö add `suggestions.stars()` and `suggestions.repos()` client methods
- `web/app/page.tsx` ŌĆö conditionally render `SuggestionsSection` vs `DiscoverySection` based on `github_login` presence

## Non-goals (future work)

# Personalized GitHub Suggestions ŌĆö Design Spec

## Overview

Add personalized repo/dependency suggestions to the dashboard for GitHub-authenticated users. Replaces the generic trending discovery carousel with two tabs: "Your Stars" (user's starred repos) and "Your Dependencies" (scan user-selected repos for dependencies). Uses public GitHub API only ŌĆö no OAuth changes needed.

## Decisions

- **Data access**: Public GitHub API via server-side `GITHUB_TOKEN` (no per-user token storage, no OAuth scope changes)
- **Placement**: Replaces trending carousel for authenticated users; unauthenticated users see existing trending carousel
- **Trigger**: On demand ŌĆö user clicks to fetch each tab's data
- **Layout**: Two tabs ŌĆö "Your Stars" | "Your Dependencies"
- **Dependencies flow**: User picks repos from checklist ŌåÆ scan selected ŌåÆ review & track results
- **Dependency scanning**: Reuses existing onboard scan pipeline (LLM extraction via `POST /api/v1/onboard/scan`)

## Known Limitations

- **Public data only**: `GET /users/{username}/starred` and `GET /users/{username}/repos` with a server-side token only return publicly visible stars and public repos. Private stars and private repos are not accessible. This is by design for the current scope ŌĆö private repo support is a future goal requiring per-user token storage.
- **Repo cap**: GitHub API caps `per_page` at 100. Users with 100+ repos see only the most recently pushed 100. Acceptable for now.

## Backend API

### Handler Construction

`NewSuggestionsHandler(httpClient *http.Client, sourcesStore db.SourcesStore, githubToken string)` ŌĆö reads `GITHUB_TOKEN` from env at initialization (same pattern as onboard scanner). Needs `SourcesStore` dependency to check which repos are already tracked.

### `GET /api/v1/suggestions/stars`

- Auth required: extracts user via `auth.UserFromContext(r.Context())`, reads `user.GitHubLogin`
- In `NO_AUTH` mode or when `github_login` is empty/`"dev"`: returns empty array (not an error)
- API key auth (Bearer token): returns 403 ŌĆö these endpoints require a session with a GitHub identity
- Fetches `GET https://api.github.com/users/{github_login}/starred` using server-side `GITHUB_TOKEN`
- Paginates up to ~100 results (GitHub returns 30/page, fetch up to 4 pages)
- Caches raw GitHub response per-user for 1 hour (keyed by `github_login`). Tracked-repo filtering applied post-cache so tracking a repo is reflected immediately without cache invalidation.
- Returns already-tracked repos with a `tracked: true` flag (not filtered out) so the frontend can dim them with a checkmark
- Response: `ApiResponse<[]SuggestionItem>` where `SuggestionItem` is `{ name, full_name, description, stars, language, url, avatar_url, provider: "github", tracked: bool }`
- Rate limit error: returns `502 Bad Gateway` with `{ error: "upstream_error", message: "GitHub API rate limit exceeded, try again later" }`

### `GET /api/v1/suggestions/repos`

- Same auth requirements as `/stars`
- Fetches `GET https://api.github.com/users/{github_login}/repos?sort=pushed&per_page=100`
- Caches per-user for 1 hour
- Response: `ApiResponse<[]RepoItem>` where `RepoItem` is `{ name, full_name, description, language, url, pushed_at }`

### Dependency scanning

Reuses existing endpoints ŌĆö no new endpoints needed:
- `POST /api/v1/onboard/scan` ŌĆö start scan for a repo
- `GET /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}` ŌĆö poll scan status
- `POST /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}/apply` ŌĆö apply selections (create projects + sources)

## Frontend Components

### SuggestionsSection

Top-level component that replaces `DiscoverySection` for users with a `github_login`. Manages tab state ("stars" | "deps"). Falls back to `DiscoverySection` when `github_login` is absent (dev mode, API key auth).

### StarsTab

- Initial state: "Load your starred repos" button
- After fetch: grid of repo cards with name, description, star count, language badge
- Already-tracked repos dimmed with checkmark (using `tracked` flag from API)
- One-click "Track" button per repo (creates project + source + auto-polls, same logic as discovery carousel)
- "Show more" pagination (client-side, data already fetched)

### DepsTab

Multi-step flow:

1. **Repo picker**: "Load your repos" button ŌåÆ fetches repo list ŌåÆ checkboxes sorted by `pushed_at` desc ŌåÆ "Scan Selected (N)" button
2. **Scanning**: Progress indicators per repo (reuses onboard progress UI patterns). Cap at 5 concurrent scans.
3. **Results**: Dependencies grouped by source repo (e.g., "From myapp ŌåÆ express, lodash, pg"). Checkboxes to select which to track. Dropdown to assign to existing project or create new one.
4. **Applied**: Success confirmation with counts (reuses onboard applied state)

## Edge Cases

- **GitHub rate limiting**: 5000 req/hr with server-side token. Cache 1hr per user. Return 502 with descriptive error if rate limited.
- **No stars / no repos**: Empty state messages with suggestion to try trending discovery or quick onboard instead.
- **Scan failures**: Per-repo status (success/failed/no deps). Failed scans don't block others.
- **Already tracked**: Stars tab returns `tracked: true` flag; frontend dims with checkmark. Deps tab results similarly indicate already-tracked dependencies.
- **Concurrent scans**: Cap at 5 concurrent per user. Existing 409 conflict handling for duplicate repo scans.
- **NO_AUTH / dev mode**: Suggestions endpoints return empty arrays when `github_login` is empty or `"dev"`. Frontend shows trending carousel fallback.
- **Cache vs. tracking**: GitHub API response is cached; tracked-repo filtering is applied post-cache so it reflects immediately.

## Files to Create/Modify

### New files
- `internal/api/suggestions.go` ŌĆö `SuggestionsHandler` with `handleStars`, `handleRepos` + GitHub API fetching + per-user caching
- `internal/api/suggestions_test.go` ŌĆö unit tests for handlers (mock GitHub API, mock SourcesStore)
- `web/components/dashboard/suggestions-section.tsx` ŌĆö top-level tabbed component
- `web/components/dashboard/stars-tab.tsx` ŌĆö stars tab with repo cards
- `web/components/dashboard/deps-tab.tsx` ŌĆö dependencies tab with multi-step flow

### Modified files
- `internal/api/server.go` ŌĆö register new `/api/v1/suggestions/` routes on auth-required chain, construct `SuggestionsHandler` with `deps.SourcesStore` and `GITHUB_TOKEN`
- `web/lib/api/client.ts` ŌĆö add `suggestions.stars()` and `suggestions.repos()` client methods
- `web/app/page.tsx` ŌĆö conditionally render `SuggestionsSection` vs `DiscoverySection` based on `github_login` presence

## Non-goals (future work)

- Private repo/star access (requires storing per-user OAuth tokens, expanded scopes)
- Auto-fetch on first login
- Recommendation ranking / ML-based suggestions
- Non-GitHub providers (GitLab, Bitbucket stars/repos)

# Personalized GitHub Suggestions ŌĆö Design Spec

## Overview

Add personalized repo/dependency suggestions to the dashboard for GitHub-authenticated users. Replaces the generic trending discovery carousel with two tabs: "Your Stars" (user's starred repos) and "Your Dependencies" (scan user-selected repos for dependencies). Uses public GitHub API only ŌĆö no OAuth changes needed.

## Decisions

- **Data access**: Public GitHub API via server-side `GITHUB_TOKEN` (no per-user token storage, no OAuth scope changes)
- **Placement**: Replaces trending carousel for authenticated users; unauthenticated users see existing trending carousel
- **Trigger**: On demand ŌĆö user clicks to fetch each tab's data
- **Layout**: Two tabs ŌĆö "Your Stars" | "Your Dependencies"
- **Dependencies flow**: User picks repos from checklist ŌåÆ scan selected ŌåÆ review & track results
- **Dependency scanning**: Reuses existing onboard scan pipeline (LLM extraction via `POST /api/v1/onboard/scan`)

## Backend API

### `GET /api/v1/suggestions/stars`

- Auth required (session cookie)
- Fetches `GET https://api.github.com/users/{github_login}/starred` using server-side `GITHUB_TOKEN`
- Paginates up to ~100 results (GitHub returns 30/page)
- Caches per-user for 1 hour (keyed by `github_login`)
- Filters out repos already tracked as sources
- Response: array of `{ name, full_name, description, stars, language, url, avatar_url, provider: "github" }`

### `GET /api/v1/suggestions/repos`

- Auth required (session cookie)
- Fetches `GET https://api.github.com/users/{github_login}/repos?sort=pushed&per_page=100`
- Caches per-user for 1 hour
- Response: array of `{ name, full_name, description, language, url, pushed_at }`

### Dependency scanning

Reuses existing endpoints ŌĆö no new endpoints needed:
- `POST /api/v1/onboard/scan` ŌĆö start scan for a repo
- `GET /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}` ŌĆö poll scan status
- `POST /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}/apply` ŌĆö apply selections (create projects + sources)

## Frontend Components

### SuggestionsSection

Top-level component that replaces `DiscoverySection` for authenticated users. Manages tab state ("stars" | "deps"). Falls back to `DiscoverySection` for unauthenticated users.

### StarsTab

- Initial state: "Load your starred repos" button
- After fetch: grid of repo cards with name, description, star count, language badge
- Already-tracked repos dimmed with checkmark
- One-click "Track" button per repo (creates project + source + auto-polls, same logic as discovery carousel)
- "Show more" pagination

### DepsTab

Multi-step flow:

1. **Repo picker**: "Load your repos" button ŌåÆ fetches repo list ŌåÆ checkboxes sorted by `pushed_at` desc ŌåÆ "Scan Selected (N)" button
2. **Scanning**: Progress indicators per repo (reuses onboard progress UI patterns). Cap at 5 concurrent scans.
3. **Results**: Dependencies grouped by source repo (e.g., "From myapp ŌåÆ express, lodash, pg"). Checkboxes to select which to track. Dropdown to assign to existing project or create new one.
4. **Applied**: Success confirmation with counts (reuses onboard applied state)

## Edge Cases

- **GitHub rate limiting**: 5000 req/hr with server-side token. Cache 1hr per user. Show friendly error if rate limited.
- **No stars / no repos**: Empty state messages with suggestion to try trending discovery or quick onboard instead.
- **Scan failures**: Per-repo status (success/failed/no deps). Failed scans don't block others.
- **Already tracked**: Both tabs filter/dim repos and dependencies already tracked as sources.
- **Concurrent scans**: Cap at 5 concurrent per user. Existing 409 conflict handling for duplicate repo scans.

## Files to Create/Modify

### New files
- `internal/api/suggestions.go` ŌĆö two handlers + GitHub API client functions + caching
- `web/components/dashboard/suggestions-section.tsx` ŌĆö top-level tabbed component
- `web/components/dashboard/stars-tab.tsx` ŌĆö stars tab with repo cards

## Non-goals (future work)

- Recommendation ranking / ML-based suggestions

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/pc/.claude/plugins/cache/superpowers-marketplace/superpowers/5.0.5/skills/writing-plans

# Writing Plans

## Overview

Write comprehensive implementation plans assuming the engineer has zero context for our codebase and questionable taste. Document everything they need to know: which files to touch for each task, code, testing, docs they might need to check, how to test it. Give them the whole plan as bite-sized tasks. DRY. YAGNI. TDD. Frequent commits.

Assume they are a skilled developer, but know almost nothing about our toolset or problem domain. Assume they don't know good test design very well.

**Announce at start:** "I'm using the writing-plans skill to create the implementation plan."

**Context:** This should be run in a dedicated worktree (created by brainstorming skill).

**Save plans to:** `docs/superpowers/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<feature-name>.md`
- (User preferences for plan location override this default)

## Scope Check

If the spec covers multiple independent subsystems, it should have been broken into sub-project specs during brainstorming. If it wasn't, suggest breaking this into separate plans ŌĆö one per subsystem. Each plan should produce working, testable software on its own.

## File Structure

Before defining tasks, map out which files will be created or modified and what each one is responsible for. This is where decomposition decisions get locked in.

- Design units with clear boundaries and well-defined interfaces. Each file should have one clear responsibility.
- You reason best about code you can hold in context at once, and your edits are more reliable when files are focused. Prefer smaller, focused files over large ones that do too much.
- Files that change together should live together. Split by responsibility, not by technical layer.
- In existing codebases, follow established patterns. If the codebase uses large files, don't unilaterally restructure - but if a file you're modifying has grown unwieldy, including a split in the plan is reasonable.

This structure informs the task decomposition. Each task should produce self-contained changes that make sense independently.

## Bite-Sized Task Granularity

**Each step is one action (2-5 minutes):**
- "Write the failing test" - step
- "Run it to make sure it fails" - step
- "Implement the minimal code to make the test pass" - step
- "Run the tests and make sure they pass" - step
- "Commit" - step

## Plan Document Header

**Every plan MUST start with this header:**

```markdown
# [Feature Name] Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** [One sentence describing what this builds]

**Architecture:** [2-3 sentences about approach]

**Tech Stack:** [Key technologies/libraries]

---
```

## Task Structure

````markdown
### Task N: [Component Name]

**Files:**
- Create: `exact/path/to/file.py`
- Modify: `exact/path/to/existing.py:123-145`
- Test: `tests/exact/path/to/test.py`

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the failing test**

```python
def test_specific_behavior():
    result = function(input)
    assert result == expected
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run test to verify it fails**

Run: `pytest tests/path/test.py::test_name -v`
Expected: FAIL with "function not defined"

- [ ] **Step 3: Write minimal implementation**

```python
def function(input):
    return expected
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Run test to verify it passes**

Run: `pytest tests/path/test.py::test_name -v`
Expected: PASS

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add tests/path/test.py src/path/file.py
git commit -m "feat: add specific feature"
```
````

## Remember
- Exact file paths always
- Complete code in plan (not "add validation")
- Exact commands with expected output
- Reference relevant skills with @ syntax
- DRY, YAGNI, TDD, frequent commits

## Plan Review Loop

After writing the complete plan:

1. Dispatch a single plan-document-reviewer subagent (see plan-document-reviewer-prompt.md) with precisely crafted review context ŌĆö never your session history. This keeps the reviewer focused on the plan, not your thought process.
   - Provide: path to the plan document, path to spec document
2. If ŌØī Issues Found: fix the issues, re-dispatch reviewer for the whole plan
3. If Ō£ģ Approved: proceed to execution handoff

**Review loop guidance:**
- Same agent that wrote the plan fixes it (preserves context)
- If loop exceeds 3 iterations, surface to human for guidance
- Reviewers are advisory ŌĆö explain disagreements if you believe feedback is incorrect

## Execution Handoff

After saving the plan, offer execution choice:

**"Plan complete and saved to `docs/superpowers/plans/<filename>.md`. Two execution options:**

**1. Subagent-Driven (recommended)** - I dispatch a fresh subagent per task, review between tasks, fast iteration

**2. Inline Execution** - Execute tasks in this session using executing-plans, batch execution with checkpoints

**Which approach?"**

**If Subagent-Driven chosen:**
- **REQUIRED SUB-SKILL:** Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development
- Fresh subagent per task + two-stage review

**If Inline Execution chosen:**
- **REQUIRED SUB-SKILL:** Use superpowers:executing-plans
- Batch execution with checkpoints for review

I'm using the writing-plans skill to create the implementation plan.

Let me first map out the file structure by reading the key files I need to reference for patterns.

input.description: Read key files for plan patterns
input.prompt:
Read and summarize the relevant patterns from these files that I need to reference when writing the implementation plan for personalized GitHub suggestions:

1. `internal/api/discover.go` ŌĆö handler construction pattern, caching approach, GitHub API calls, response format
2. `internal/api/server.go` ŌĆö route registration pattern, dependency injection, auth chain setup
3. `web/components/dashboard/discovery-section.tsx` ŌĆö component structure, one-click track logic, API calls
4. `web/lib/api/client.ts` ŌĆö API client method pattern
5. `web/app/page.tsx` ŌĆö how DiscoverySection is rendered, auth context usage

1ŌåÆpackage api
     2ŌåÆ
     3ŌåÆimport (
     4ŌåÆ	"net/http"
     5ŌåÆ	"strings"
     6ŌåÆ
     7ŌåÆ	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
     8ŌåÆ	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/auth"
     9ŌåÆ	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/ingestion"
    10ŌåÆ	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/routing"
    11ŌåÆ)
    12ŌåÆ
    13ŌåÆ// Dependencies holds all external dependencies required for registering API routes.
    14ŌåÆtype Dependencies struct {
    15ŌåÆ	DB                    *pgxpool.Pool
    16ŌåÆ	ProjectsStore         ProjectsStore
    17ŌåÆ	ReleasesStore         ReleasesStore
    18ŌåÆ	SubscriptionsStore    SubscriptionsStore
    19ŌåÆ	SourcesStore          SourcesStore
    20ŌåÆ	ChannelsStore         ChannelsStore
    21ŌåÆ	ContextSourcesStore   ContextSourcesStore
    22ŌåÆ	SemanticReleasesStore SemanticReleasesStore
    23ŌåÆ	AgentStore            AgentStore
    24ŌåÆ	TodosStore            TodosStore
    25ŌåÆ	OnboardStore          OnboardStore
    26ŌåÆ	PublicURL             string
    27ŌåÆ	KeyStore              KeyStore
    28ŌåÆ	SessionValidator      SessionValidator
    29ŌåÆ	HealthChecker         HealthChecker
    30ŌåÆ	Broadcaster           *Broadcaster
    31ŌåÆ	NoAuth                bool
    32ŌåÆ	IngestionService      *ingestion.Service
    33ŌåÆ	HTTPClient            *http.Client
    34ŌåÆ}
    35ŌåÆ
    36ŌåÆ// RegisterRoutes registers all API v1 routes on the given ServeMux.
    37ŌåÆfunc RegisterRoutes(mux *http.ServeMux, deps Dependencies) {
    38ŌåÆ	// Public chain: no auth required.
    39ŌåÆ	publicChain := Chain(RequestID, Logger, Recovery)
    40ŌåÆ
    41ŌåÆ	// Authenticated chain: includes auth unless NoAuth is set.
    42ŌåÆ	chain := publicChain
    43ŌåÆ	if !deps.NoAuth {
    44ŌåÆ		chain = Chain(RequestID, Logger, Recovery, RateLimit(10, 20), Auth(deps.KeyStore, deps.SessionValidator))
    45ŌåÆ	}
    46ŌåÆ
    47ŌåÆ	// Projects (CRUD)
    48ŌåÆ	projects := NewProjectsHandler(deps.ProjectsStore)
    49ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.List)))
    50ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Create)))
    51ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Get)))
    52ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Update)))
    53ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Delete)))
    54ŌåÆ
    55ŌåÆ	// Sources (nested under projects)
    56ŌåÆ	sources := NewSourcesHandler(deps.SourcesStore, deps.IngestionService, deps.HTTPClient)
    57ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.List)))
    58ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Create)))
    59ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Get)))
    60ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Update)))
    61ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Delete)))
    62ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/sources/{id}/poll", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.FetchReleases)))
    63ŌåÆ
    64ŌåÆ	// Context Sources (nested under projects)
    65ŌåÆ	contextSources := NewContextSourcesHandler(deps.ContextSourcesStore)
    66ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/context-sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.List)))
    67ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/context-sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Create)))
    68ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Get)))
    69ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Update)))
    70ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Delete)))
    71ŌåÆ
    72ŌåÆ	// Releases (read-only, nested under sources and projects)
    73ŌåÆ	releases := NewReleasesHandler(deps.ReleasesStore)
    74ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.List)))
    75ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/sources/{id}/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.ListBySource)))
    76ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.ListByProject)))
    77ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.Get)))
    78ŌåÆ
    79ŌåÆ	// Semantic Releases (nested under projects + top-level list)
    80ŌåÆ	semanticReleases := NewSemanticReleasesHandler(deps.SemanticReleasesStore)
    81ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.ListAll)))
    82ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/semantic-releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.List)))
    83ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.Get)))
    84ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.ListSources)))
    85ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.Delete)))
    86ŌåÆ
    87ŌåÆ	// Subscriptions (CRUD)
    88ŌåÆ	subscriptions := NewSubscriptionsHandler(deps.SubscriptionsStore)
    89ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/subscriptions", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.List)))
    90ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/subscriptions", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Create)))
    91ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/subscriptions/batch", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.BatchCreate)))
    92ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/subscriptions/batch", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.BatchDelete)))
    93ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Get)))
    94ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Update)))
    95ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Delete)))
    96ŌåÆ
    97ŌåÆ	// Channels (CRUD + test)
    98ŌåÆ	channels := NewChannelsHandler(deps.ChannelsStore, routing.NewSenders(), deps.PublicURL)
    99ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/channels", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.List)))
   100ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/channels", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Create)))
   101ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Get)))
   102ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Update)))
   103ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Delete)))
   104ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/channels/{id}/test", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Test)))
   105ŌåÆ
   106ŌåÆ	// Agent
   107ŌåÆ	agent := NewAgentHandler(deps.AgentStore)
   108ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/agent/run", chain(http.HandlerFunc(agent.TriggerRun)))
   109ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/agent/runs", chain(http.HandlerFunc(agent.ListRuns)))
   110ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/agent-runs/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(agent.GetRun)))
   111ŌåÆ
   112ŌåÆ	// Todos
   113ŌåÆ	todos := NewTodosHandler(deps.TodosStore, deps.PublicURL)
   114ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/todos", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.List)))
   115ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/todos/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.Get)))
   116ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("PATCH /api/v1/todos/{id}/acknowledge", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.Acknowledge)))
   117ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("PATCH /api/v1/todos/{id}/resolve", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.Resolve)))
   118ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("PATCH /api/v1/todos/{id}/reopen", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.Reopen)))
   119ŌåÆ	// One-click endpoints for notification links (GET so they work as <a href>)
   120ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/todos/{id}/acknowledge", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.Acknowledge)))
   121ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/todos/{id}/resolve", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.Resolve)))
   122ŌåÆ
   123ŌåÆ	// Onboard (repo dependency scanning)
   124ŌåÆ	ob := NewOnboardHandler(deps.OnboardStore)
   125ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/onboard/scan", chain(http.HandlerFunc(ob.Scan)))
   126ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(ob.GetScan)))
   127ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}/apply", chain(http.HandlerFunc(ob.Apply)))
   128ŌåÆ
   129ŌåÆ	// Providers (metadata ŌĆö static, no store needed)
   130ŌåÆ	providers := NewProvidersHandler()
   131ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/providers", chain(http.HandlerFunc(providers.List)))
   132ŌåÆ
   133ŌåÆ	// Discovery (public ŌĆö no auth, proxies external APIs)
   134ŌåÆ	discover := NewDiscoverHandler(deps.HTTPClient, "", "")
   135ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/discover/github", publicChain(http.HandlerFunc(discover.GitHub)))
   136ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/discover/dockerhub", publicChain(http.HandlerFunc(discover.DockerHub)))
   137ŌåÆ
   138ŌåÆ	// SSE events
   139ŌåÆ	events := NewEventsHandler(deps.Broadcaster)
   140ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/events", chain(http.HandlerFunc(events.Stream)))
   141ŌåÆ
   142ŌåÆ	// Health (public ŌĆö no auth middleware)
   143ŌåÆ	health := NewHealthHandler(deps.HealthChecker)
   144ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/health", publicChain(http.HandlerFunc(health.Check)))
   145ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/stats", chain(http.HandlerFunc(health.Stats)))
   146ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/stats/trend", chain(http.HandlerFunc(health.Trend)))
   147ŌåÆ}
   148ŌåÆ
   149ŌåÆ// RegisterAuthRoutes registers OAuth and session endpoints on the mux.
   150ŌåÆ// frontendURL is used in noAuth mode to redirect back to the frontend (may be empty).
   151ŌåÆfunc RegisterAuthRoutes(mux *http.ServeMux, oauthHandler *auth.OAuthHandler, noAuth bool, frontendURL string) {
   152ŌåÆ	if noAuth {
   153ŌåÆ		mux.HandleFunc("GET /auth/me", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   154ŌåÆ			if c, err := r.Cookie("dev_logged_out"); err == nil && c.Value == "1" {
   155ŌåÆ				http.Error(w, `{"error":"unauthorized"}`, http.StatusUnauthorized)
   156ŌåÆ				return
   157ŌåÆ			}
   158ŌåÆ			auth.HandleMeDev(w, r)
   159ŌåÆ		})
   160ŌåÆ		mux.HandleFunc("POST /auth/logout", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   161ŌåÆ			http.SetCookie(w, &http.Cookie{
   162ŌåÆ				Name:  "dev_logged_out",
   163ŌåÆ				Value: "1",
   164ŌåÆ				Path:  "/",
   165ŌåÆ			})
   166ŌåÆ			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
   167ŌåÆ		})
   168ŌåÆ		mux.HandleFunc("GET /auth/github", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   169ŌåÆ			// Clear the logout cookie so dev user is "logged in" again
   170ŌåÆ			http.SetCookie(w, &http.Cookie{
   171ŌåÆ				Name:   "dev_logged_out",
   172ŌåÆ				Value:  "",
   173ŌåÆ				Path:   "/",
   174ŌåÆ				MaxAge: -1,
   175ŌåÆ			})
   176ŌåÆ			target := "/"
   177ŌåÆ			if frontendURL != "" {
   178ŌåÆ				target = strings.TrimRight(frontendURL, "/") + "/"
   179ŌåÆ			}
   180ŌåÆ			http.Redirect(w, r, target, http.StatusFound)
   181ŌåÆ		})
   182ŌåÆ		return
   183ŌåÆ	}
   184ŌåÆ
   185ŌåÆ	sessionMw := oauthHandler.RequireSession
   186ŌåÆ	mux.HandleFunc("GET /auth/github", oauthHandler.HandleGitHubRedirect)
   187ŌåÆ	mux.HandleFunc("GET /auth/github/callback", oauthHandler.HandleGitHubCallback)
   188ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /auth/me", sessionMw(http.HandlerFunc(oauthHandler.HandleMe)))
   189ŌåÆ	mux.HandleFunc("POST /auth/logout", oauthHandler.HandleLogout)
   190ŌåÆ}

    11ŌåÆfunc NewPool(ctx context.Context, databaseURL string) (*pgxpool.Pool, error) {

    18ŌåÆ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("ping: %w", err)

    36ŌåÆ// RegisterRoutes registers all API v1 routes on the given ServeMux.

    42ŌåÆ	chain := publicChain

    45ŌåÆ	}

    47ŌåÆ	// Projects (CRUD)

    51ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Get)))

    54ŌåÆ
    55ŌåÆ	// Sources (nested under projects)
    56ŌåÆ	sources := NewSourcesHandler(deps.SourcesStore, deps.IngestionService, deps.HTTPClient)

    71ŌåÆ
    72ŌåÆ	// Releases (read-only, nested under sources and projects)

    86ŌåÆ
    87ŌåÆ	// Subscriptions (CRUD)

    96ŌåÆ
    97ŌåÆ	// Channels (CRUD + test)
    98ŌåÆ	channels := NewChannelsHandler(deps.ChannelsStore, routing.NewSenders(), deps.PublicURL)
    99ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/channels", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.List)))
   100ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/channels", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Create)))
   101ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Get)))

   106ŌåÆ	// Agent
   107ŌåÆ	agent := NewAgentHandler(deps.AgentStore)

   119ŌåÆ	// One-click endpoints for notification links (GET so they work as <a href>)

   121ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/todos/{id}/resolve", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.Resolve)))
   122ŌåÆ
   123ŌåÆ	// Onboard (repo dependency scanning)
   124ŌåÆ	ob := NewOnboardHandler(deps.OnboardStore)
   125ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/onboard/scan", chain(http.HandlerFunc(ob.Scan)))
   126ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(ob.GetScan)))
   127ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}/apply", chain(http.HandlerFunc(ob.Apply)))
   128ŌåÆ
   129ŌåÆ	// Providers (metadata ŌĆö static, no store needed)
   130ŌåÆ	providers := NewProvidersHandler()
   131ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/providers", chain(http.HandlerFunc(providers.List)))
   132ŌåÆ
   133ŌåÆ	// Discovery (public ŌĆö no auth, proxies external APIs)
   134ŌåÆ	discover := NewDiscoverHandler(deps.HTTPClient, "", "")
   135ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/discover/github", publicChain(http.HandlerFunc(discover.GitHub)))
   136ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/discover/dockerhub", publicChain(http.HandlerFunc(discover.DockerHub)))
   137ŌåÆ
   138ŌåÆ	// SSE events
   139ŌåÆ	events := NewEventsHandler(deps.Broadcaster)
   140ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/events", chain(http.HandlerFunc(events.Stream)))
   141ŌåÆ
   142ŌåÆ	// Health (public ŌĆö no auth middleware)
   143ŌåÆ	health := NewHealthHandler(deps.HealthChecker)
   144ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/health", publicChain(http.HandlerFunc(health.Check)))
   145ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/stats", chain(http.HandlerFunc(health.Stats)))
   146ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/stats/trend", chain(http.HandlerFunc(health.Trend)))

   148ŌåÆ
   149ŌåÆ// RegisterAuthRoutes registers OAuth and session endpoints on the mux.
   150ŌåÆ// frontendURL is used in noAuth mode to redirect back to the frontend (may be empty).
   151ŌåÆfunc RegisterAuthRoutes(mux *http.ServeMux, oauthHandler *auth.OAuthHandler, noAuth bool, frontendURL string) {
   152ŌåÆ	if noAuth {
   153ŌåÆ		mux.HandleFunc("GET /auth/me", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   154ŌåÆ			if c, err := r.Cookie("dev_logged_out"); err == nil && c.Value == "1" {
   155ŌåÆ				http.Error(w, `{"error":"unauthorized"}`, http.StatusUnauthorized)
   156ŌåÆ				return
   157ŌåÆ			}
   158ŌåÆ			auth.HandleMeDev(w, r)
   159ŌåÆ		})
   160ŌåÆ		mux.HandleFunc("POST /auth/logout", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   161ŌåÆ			http.SetCookie(w, &http.Cookie{
   162ŌåÆ				Name:  "dev_logged_out",
   163ŌåÆ				Value: "1",
   164ŌåÆ				Path:  "/",
   165ŌåÆ			})
   166ŌåÆ			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
   167ŌåÆ		})
   168ŌåÆ		mux.HandleFunc("GET /auth/github", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   169ŌåÆ			// Clear the logout cookie so dev user is "logged in" again
   170ŌåÆ			http.SetCookie(w, &http.Cookie{
   171ŌåÆ				Name:   "dev_logged_out",
   172ŌåÆ				Value:  "",
   173ŌåÆ				Path:   "/",
   174ŌåÆ				MaxAge: -1,
   175ŌåÆ			})
   176ŌåÆ			target := "/"
   177ŌåÆ			if frontendURL != "" {
   178ŌåÆ				target = strings.TrimRight(frontendURL, "/") + "/"
   179ŌåÆ			}
   180ŌåÆ			http.Redirect(w, r, target, http.StatusFound)
   181ŌåÆ		})
   182ŌåÆ		return
   183ŌåÆ	}
   184ŌåÆ
   185ŌåÆ	sessionMw := oauthHandler.RequireSession
   186ŌåÆ	mux.HandleFunc("GET /auth/github", oauthHandler.HandleGitHubRedirect)
   187ŌåÆ	mux.HandleFunc("GET /auth/github/callback", oauthHandler.HandleGitHubCallback)
   188ŌåÆ	mux.Handle("GET /auth/me", sessionMw(http.HandlerFunc(oauthHandler.HandleMe)))
   189ŌåÆ	mux.HandleFunc("POST /auth/logout", oauthHandler.HandleLogout)
   190ŌåÆ}
